rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dual trifluoromethylation/cyclization of 2-aryl-N-acryloyl indoles with CF3SO2Na to afford bis-trifluoromethylated indolo[2,1-a]isoquinolinones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na to a trifluoromethanesulfonyl radical that loses SO2 to give CF3 radical; later anodic oxidations of cyclized C-radicals generate cationic intermediates that lose H+ to rearomatize/form product.

key_intermediates.name: carbon radical A (CF3 adduct on acryloyl C=C)
cyclized C3-indolyl radical B (after intramolecular radical addition onto indole C3)
benzylic radical D after second CF3• addition

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2− to CF3SO2• which extrudes SO2 to give CF3•; CF3• adds to the acryloyl alkene of 1a, the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the indole C3 to give radical B, which is anodically oxidized to cation C and deprotonated to neutral mono-CF3 intermediate 5; a second CF3• adds to the styrene-like alkene of 5 to give benzylic radical D, anodic oxidation to cation E followed by deprotonation furnishes 3a.
rate_determining_step: Intramolecular radical cyclization of A onto the indole (TS2, ΔG‡ = 19.1 kcal/mol).

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed; DPE-CF3SO2 adduct 4 detected by ESI-MS, implicating CF3SO2• as a radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: Without electricity essentially no reaction occurred; electricity is indispensable.

probe_reagents.name: reaction quench at 2 h
probe_reagents.observation: Mono-CF3 intermediate 5 detected by ESI-MS, supporting stepwise dual trifluoromethylation.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 2a vs 1a
probe_reagents.observation: CF3SO2Na oxidation onset at 1.80 V vs Ag/AgCl, lower than 1a (1.97 V), indicating preferential anodic oxidation of CF3SO2− to generate CF3•.

notes: Mechanism supported by DFT (wB97XD/def2TZVPP, CPCM-MeCN) including TS1 (CF3• addition, 10.5 kcal/mol), TS2 (radical cyclization, 19.1 kcal/mol, rate-determining), TS3 (second CF3• addition, 9.1 kcal/mol), and Hirshfeld spin-density analysis tracking spin-center migration from CF3 carbon to β-carbon during each addition/cyclization step. Single-crystal X-ray of 3a confirms regiochemistry. Substrate with CHF2SO2Na failed, suggesting sensitivity to radical stability of the perfluoroalkyl source.
